title: ci: fix codspeed segfault
body_text: Pin Python to 3.13.11 in the CodSpeed benchmark job. CPython 3.13.12 introduced a segfault in CodSpeed's walltime callgraph post-processing ([CodSpeedHQ/pytest-codspeed#106](https://github.com/CodSpeedHQ/pytest-codspeed/issues/106)), causing all `libs/core` benchmark runs to fail with `exit status: 139` since Feb 26 despite all 13 benchmarks passing.

entities.description: A benchmarking tool used in the CI pipeline.

entities.name: CPython 3.13.12
entities.description: A specific version of the CPython interpreter that introduced a bug.

entities.description: A component within CodSpeed responsible for processing callgraph data related to walltime.

entities.name: libs/core
entities.description: A library or core component of the project whose benchmark runs were failing.

entities.name: Benchmark Runs
entities.description: The execution of performance benchmarks for the project.

entities.name: Exit Status 139
entities.description: A specific exit status code indicating a segmentation fault.

entities.name: pytest-codspeed#106
entities.description: An external GitHub issue tracking the segfault in pytest-codspeed.

entities.name: CPython 3.13.11
entities.description: A specific version of the CPython interpreter used to fix the segfault.

relations.evidence: segfault in CodSpeed's walltime callgraph post-processing

relations.label: CAUSES

relations.label: USES
relations.evidence: Pin Python to 3.13.11 in the CodSpeed benchmark job.

extracted_data.summary: A CPython 3.13.12 segfault in CodSpeed's walltime callgraph post-processing caused `libs/core` benchmark runs to fail with exit status 139, leading to a fix by pinning Python to 3.13.11.
